com:Annotation.com:Note: Help points at this station are available for customers to use 24/7

Help points can be found at the following location(s):

  • Platform 1 (for trains towards Norwich) - Between the covered waiting area and the footbridge, fixed to the grey post beneath the customer information screen
  • Platform 2 (for trains towards Great Yarmouth) - Under the canopy, fixed to the platform-facing wall of the former station building

The hours shown are for the Customer Relations team on 0345 600 7245 (option 8).

Monday-Saturday 08:00-20:00Sunday 10:00-20:00Bank Holidays 09:00-18:00

Closed on Christmas Day and Boxing Day.
Find WiFi Hotspots around Acle station

We want everyone to travel with confidence. That is why, if you are planning on travelling on national rail services, you can request an assistance booking in advance - now up to 2 hours before your journey is due to start, any time of the day. For more information about Passenger Assist and how to request an assistance booking via Passenger Assist, please click here.

This station is not staffed. Assistance can be provided by the conductor on board, but booking is recommended to avoid any delay to your journey.

What assistance is available for customers using this station?

  • Board and alight
  • Ramp assistance
  • Luggage (on and off train only)
  • Ticket sale on board

How can Turn Up And Go (TUAG) assistance be requested by customers using this station?

  • By calling 0345 600 7245 (option 4)
  • By selecting the help button on the ticket machine at this station
  • By using one of the two Help Points at this station

Assistance meeting point is on the platform.
Details of nearest taxis are shown on station information poster

There is step free access from the carpark to Platform 2 (for trains towards Great Yarmouth) only, with no step free access from the carpark to Platform 1 (for trains towards Norwich) as there is a stepped footbridge between the platforms. Step free access is available to Platform 1 via a footpath from Reedham Road, although it is approximately 250 metres from the roadside to the platform.

This station is a category B3 according to ORR station classification system.

https://www.orr.gov.uk/media/10955

Assistance meeting point is on the platform.

Location.com:Note: There are two cycle stands on Platform 1 (for trains towards Norwich), and a sheltered cycle parking area providing ten spaces can be accessed from the station car park, located to the left of the former station buildings behind Platform 2 (for trains towards Great Yarmouth).

com:Annotation.com:Note: Rail replacement bus services pick up and drop off in Norwich Road, in front of the Police Station.
Help points at this station are available for customers to use 24/7

Help points at this station can be found at the following location(s):

  • Platform 1 (for trains towards Colchester, Chelmsford, London) - fixed to the wall to the right of the ticket machine at the entrance to this platform

SmartcardComments.com:Note: Greater Anglia Smartcards can be obtained from www.greateranglia.co.uk/smart

com:Annotation.com:Note:
Monday-Saturday 08:00-20:00Sunday 10:00-20:00Bank Holidays 09:00-18:00

The hours shown are for the Customer Relations team on 0345 600 7245 (option 8).

Closed on Christmas Day and Boxing Day.
Find WiFi Hotspots around Alresford station

We want everyone to travel with confidence. That is why, if you are planning on travelling on national rail services, you can request an assistance booking in advance - now up to 2 hours before your journey is due to start, any time of the day. For more information about Passenger Assist and how to request an assistance booking via Passenger Assist, please click here.

This station is not staffed. Assistance can be provided by the conductor on board, but booking is recommended to avoid any delay to your journey.

What assistance is available for customers using this station?

  • Board and alight
  • Ramp assistance
  • Luggage (on and off train only)
  • Ticket sale on board

How can Turn Up And Go (TUAG) assistance be requested by customers using this station?

  • By calling 0345 600 7245 (option 4)
  • By selecting the help button on the ticket machine at this station
  • By using the Help Point at this station

Assistance meeting point is on the platform, next to the yellow boarding ramp.
Details of nearest taxis are shown on station information poster

This station has step free access to both platforms from Station Road, with approximately 20 metres between the two entrances which are either side of the level crossing.

This station is a category B1 station according to ORR station classification system. https://www.orr.gov.uk/media/10955

Assistance meeting point is on the platform, next to the yellow boarding ramp.

Location.com:Note: There are five cycle stands on Platform 1 (for trains towards Colchester, Chelmsford, and London), located underneath the footbridge steps. Another five cycle stands are next to the waiting shelter on Platform 2 (for trains towards Clacton and Walton-on-the-Naze).

com:Annotation.com:Note: Rail replacement bus services pick up and drop off on Station Road, either side of the level crossing.
Local Buses to Colchester, Clacton and Brightlingsea

SmartcardComments.com:Note: Tickets can be collected on Smartcard at this station

com:Annotation.com:Note:
Monday-Saturday 08:00-20:00Sunday 10:00-20:00Bank Holidays 09:00-18:00

The hours shown are for the Customer Relations team on 0345 600 7245 (option 8).

Closed on Christmas Day and Boxing Day.
Find WiFi Hotspots around Althorne station

We want everyone to travel with confidence. That is why, if you are planning on travelling on national rail services, you can request an assistance booking in advance - now up to 2 hours before your journey is due to start, any time of the day. For more information about Passenger Assist and how to request an assistance booking via Passenger Assist, please click here.

Some services operate with a guard who can provide assistance, however this would not be available if the train was in driver only operation. Mobile assistance teams operate within the area and can be available for booked assistance between 0600 and 2100 Mon-Sat and 0800-2000 Sundays. Booking is recommended. Outside of these times alternative transport will be provided (via the help point in the instance of turn up and go).
Details of nearest taxis are shown on station information poster

Single platform station. Step free access to platform

This station is a category A station according to ORR station classification system. https://www.orr.gov.uk/media/10955

Assistance meeting point is the platform

CarPark.Name: Station Car Park
CarPark.AccessibleSpacesNote: Parking is free for disabled customers displaying a valid International Blue Badge

com:Annotation.com:Note: Pick up and drop off on Fords of Athorne forecourt.
Local bus services three minutes walk from station, junction of Fambridge Road.
Help points at this station are available for customers to use 24/7

Help points can be found at the following location(s):

  • Platform 1 (for trains towards Wymondham, Norwich) - Fixed to the platform-facing wall of the former station building, under the blue 'Information' sign
  • Platform 2 (for trains towards Thetford, Ely, Cambridge) - Fixed to the inside wall to the right of the covered waiting area, under the canopy

Monday-Saturday 08:00-20:00Sunday 10:00-20:00Bank Holidays 09:00-18:00

The hours shown are for the Customer Relations team on 0345 600 7245 (option 8).

Closed on Christmas Day and Boxing Day.
Find WiFi Hotspots around Attleborough station

We want everyone to travel with confidence. That is why, if you are planning on travelling on national rail services, you can request an assistance booking in advance - now up to 2 hours before your journey is due to start, any time of the day. For more information about Passenger Assist and how to request an assistance booking via Passenger Assist, please click here.

This station is not staffed. Assistance can be provided by the conductor on board, but booking is recommended to avoid any delay to your journey.

What assistance is available for customers using this station?

  • Board and alight
  • Ramp assistance
  • Luggage (on and off train only)
  • Ticket sale on board

How can Turn Up And Go (TUAG) assistance be requested by customers using this station?

  • By calling 0345 600 7245 (option 4)
  • By selecting the help button on the ticket machine at this station
  • By using one of the two Help Points at this station

Assistance meeting point is on the platform, next to the yellow boarding ramp.
Details of nearest taxis are shown on station information poster

This station has step free access to both platforms but from different locations on Station Road, with approximately 30-40 metres between the entrances to each platform from the roadside. The station is split by a busy road with a platform on either side.

This station is a category B1 station according to ORR station classification system. https://www.orr.gov.uk/media/10955

Assistance meeting point is on the platform, next to the yellow boarding ramp.

Location.com:Note: There are four cycle stands that can be accessed from the station car park, located to the left of the former station buildings behind Platform 1 (for trains towards Wymondham and Norwich). A sheltered cycle parking area providing five cycle stands is on Platform 2 (for trains towards Thetford, Ely, and Cambridge), between the entrance to this platform and the canopy.

CarPark.Name: Station Car Park
CarPark.AccessibleSpacesNote: Parking is free for disabled customers displaying a valid International Blue Badge

com:Annotation.com:Note: Rail replacement bus services pick up and drop off on Station Road, by the entrance to the station car park.
Help point available on platform 1

SmartcardComments.com:Note: Greater Anglia Smartcards can be obtained from www.greateranglia.co.uk/smart

com:Annotation.com:Note:
Monday-Saturday 08:00-20:00Sunday 10:00-20:00Bank Holidays 09:00-18:00

The hours shown are for the Customer Relations team on 0345 600 7245 (option 8).

Closed on Christmas Day and Boxing Day.

com:Location.com:Note: Waiting rooms are available on platforms 1 and 2
Toilets are located in the booking hall and are open when the Ticket Office is open

com:Annotation.com:Note: News agent

We want everyone to travel with confidence. That is why, if you are planning on travelling on national rail services, you can request an assistance booking in advance - now up to 2 hours before your journey is due to start, any time of the day. For more information about Passenger Assist and how to request an assistance booking via Passenger Assist, please click here.

Driver only operated services. Assistance at this station is provided by a staff member during ticket office opening hours. Outside of these hours it is recommended that customers use Bishops Stortford (12 miles south) for journeys towards London. An appropriate accessible taxi will be supplied for journeys to intermediate unstaffed stations. Booking is recommended
Details of nearest taxis are shown on station information poster

This station has step free access to platforms via lifts.

This station is a category A station according to ORR station classification system.

https://www.orr.gov.uk/media/10955

Assistance meeting point is the ticket office
Substitute bus services stop at front of Station
In front of station.
Local buses at front of station
